stories.headline: Quick Queries Make Reporter's Life Easier
stories.summary: Kenton Robinson shares his experiences using online services and ProfNet to streamline reporting, emphasizing the efficiency and breadth of information available for journalism.

stories.headline: Fed or Med? You can find it online
stories.summary: FedWorld, a government online database launched in 1993, has become a useful resource for journalists covering the White House and federal issues, while the Internet also offers specialized medical newsletters on topics like RSI, Lyme Disease, and cancer that can help reporters develop stories.
